failedExamples.trajectory: If the bridge collapsed, then the village would flood. The bridge collapsed at dawn, and by noon the village was underwater. The elders had warned them: only if they reinforced the dam could they survive. They had not reinforced the dam. There was no surviving this.
failedExamples.feedback: [PropositionalChecker/contradiction] Contradiction (P ∧ ¬P): "Only if they reinforced the dam could they survive" (sentence 3) contradicts "There was no surviving this" (sentence 5)., [DeonticChecker/broken_obligation] Broken obligation: villagers was obligated to "reinforce_dam" (elders), but failed without narrative consequence or acknowledgment.

failedExamples.trajectory: Elaria knew the rule of the Ashen Peaks: if you carry the Sunblade, then the shadow creatures cannot touch you. She gripped the Sunblade tightly as the shadows circled. They lunged but recoiled, unable to breach the blade's radiance. The rule held true.
failedExamples.feedback: [PropositionalChecker/modus_ponens_violation] Modus Ponens violation: rule "r1" states "Elaria carries the Sunblade" → "Shadow creatures are unable to breach the blade's radiance", but the antecedent is true while the consequent is false., [PropositionalChecker/modus_tollens_violation] Modus Tollens violation: rule "r1" states "Elaria carries the Sunblade" → "Shadow creatures are unable to breach the blade's radiance". Since "Shadow creatures are unable to breach the blade's radiance" is false, "Elaria carries the Sunblade" should also be false, but it is true., [EpistemicChecker/psychic_knowledge] Psychic knowledge: "Elaria" knows "The rule of the Ashen Peaks regarding the Sunblade" but the source is unexplained. The agent acts on information with no narrative justification., [CausalChecker/necessary_precondition_missing] Necessary precondition may be missing: "Sunblade protects wielder from shadow creatures" (source: lore). Related events found but the required precondition is not evidenced.

failedExamples.trajectory: If the seal is broken, then the demon escapes. The seal remained intact, glowing with ancient power. The priests breathed easier—the demon remained imprisoned. Their vigil had not been in vain.
failedExamples.feedback: [PropositionalChecker/contradiction] Contradiction (P ∧ ¬P): "The seal is broken" (sentence 1) contradicts "The seal remained intact" (sentence 2)., [PropositionalChecker/contradiction] Contradiction (P ∧ ¬P): "The demon escapes" (sentence 1) contradicts "The demon remained imprisoned" (sentence 3).

failedExamples.trajectory: The oracle spoke in absolutes: 'Only a descendant of the First King can wield the Crown of Stars.' [CHARACTER_NAME_MAREN] was no descendant of the First King—she knew her lineage traced to common farmers. She passed the crown to [CHARACTER_NAME_ALDRIC], whose bloodline was true, and he placed it upon his brow.
failedExamples.feedback: [EpistemicChecker/psychic_knowledge] Psychic knowledge: "[CHARACTER_NAME_MAREN]" knows "[CHARACTER_NAME_MAREN] is not a descendant of the First King" but the source is unexplained. The agent acts on information with no narrative justification.

failedExamples.trajectory: Neither the northern road nor the southern pass was safe. Bandits controlled the north; rockslides had sealed the south. With both routes blocked, they had no choice but to take the treacherous mountain trail—the only remaining path.
failedExamples.feedback: [PropositionalChecker/modus_ponens_violation] Modus Ponens violation: rule "r1" states "Bandits control the north" → "The northern road is safe", but the antecedent is true while the consequent is false., [PropositionalChecker/modus_tollens_violation] Modus Tollens violation: rule "r1" states "Bandits control the north" → "The northern road is safe". Since "The northern road is safe" is false, "Bandits control the north" should also be false, but it is true., [PropositionalChecker/modus_ponens_violation] Modus Ponens violation: rule "r2" states "Rockslides sealed the south" → "The southern pass is safe", but the antecedent is true while the consequent is false., [PropositionalChecker/modus_tollens_violation] Modus Tollens violation: rule "r2" states "Rockslides sealed the south" → "The southern pass is safe". Since "The southern pass is safe" is false, "Rockslides sealed the south" should also be false, but it is true., [EpistemicChecker/psychic_knowledge] Psychic knowledge: "they" knows "the mountain trail is the only path" but the source is unexplained. The agent acts on information with no narrative justification., [CausalChecker/necessary_precondition_missing] Necessary precondition may be missing: "If all other paths are blocked, the remaining path must be taken" (source: narrative). Related events found but the required precondition is not evidenced.
